one_time_description: Agricultural Benefits Settlement expense of $673,835 (prior year $1,532,918)
economic_notes: Nation has investments in oil and gas, oilfield construction, industry relations, and investment management board. Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust holds ~$30M in portfolio investments. Treaty Land Entitlement claim in progress.
notes: Audit is qualified on two bases: (1) investments in related First Nation entities use modified equity method rather than full consolidation, and financial records for several entities were unavailable; (2) asset retirement obligations have not been estimated or recorded. Accumulated surplus per balance sheet differs from statement of operations end-of-year figure ($71,234,408 vs $69,026,035) due to remeasurement losses and other equity components. Five entities accounted for by modified equity method (GBEs). One entity (Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust) fully consolidated. Several subsidiary financial statements were unavailable at audit completion date. The accumulated surplus on the statement of financial position is $69,026,035 while the statement of operations shows $71,234,408 — the difference reflects remeasurement losses of $(2,208,373). ISC funding flows through Western Cree Tribal Council. Prior year figures restated per Note 23.
